title: Make GitHub repo explorable
description: Add README exploration links, demo flow, SVG architecture asset, contributing guide, MIT license, changelog, repository metadata spec, and label spec.
close_reason: Added README exploration links, demo documentation, SVG flow visual, contributing guide, MIT license, changelog, repository metadata spec, and label spec. CLI auth blocked live GitHub metadata mutation, so desired settings are versioned in .github/.

title: Make README impact-focused
description: Refocus README around Patchpilot product impact and reduce assignment framing to a concise validation/evaluation note.
close_reason: README now leads with product impact, keeps assignment/eval details as a concise validation note, and highlights performance bottleneck impact.

title: Rename app to Patchpilot
description: Update product/display name from Back To Service to Patchpilot while preserving existing back-to-service protocol handles for compatibility.
close_reason: Renamed visible product/app references to Patchpilot, updated package identity and CLI usage, preserved legacy protocol handles for compatibility, and passed quality gates.

title: Add Sentry performance bottleneck recovery
description: Implement Sentry performance bottleneck intake, GitHub performance incident sync, repair dispatch payloads, performance verification checks, config, docs, and tests.
close_reason: Implemented Sentry performance bottleneck intake, GitHub performance issue sync, repair dispatch payloads, config/docs, and verification checks. Quality gates pass.

title: Add OpenAI Codex repair workflow provider
description: Add a Codex/OpenAI target-repo repair workflow template and provider-facing docs/scripts so Back To Service can dispatch a cheaper Codex repair worker alongside the existing Claude workflow.
close_reason: Implemented Codex repair provider workflow and dispatch selection.

title: Map Sentry frames to suspect files
description: Add token-saving suspect-file mapping from Sentry stack frames plus SQLite memory, persist ranked file hints, include them in Claude dispatch/status context, and update the target workflow prompt to inspect suspect files before broad repo search.
close_reason: Implemented Sentry stack/memory suspect-file mapping, persisted ranked file hints in SQLite memory, included suspect files in dispatch/status context, updated Claude workflow prompt, docs, and tests.

title: Add SQLite incident memory for Sentry lessons
description: Implement SQLite-first compact incident memory for Sentry-backed GitHub issues: redacted memory records, similarity retrieval, advisory prompt formatting, agent-loop integration, synthetic examples, and tests.
close_reason: Implemented SQLite incident memory with redacted compact lessons, similarity retrieval, advisory memory context in agent loop, synthetic eval fixtures, docs, and tests.

title: Implement lean GitHub-issue-first roadmap
description: Reshape Back To Service around Sentry-created GitHub issues as the entrypoint, updating planning docs, docs, agent fixtures/evals, and watcher parsing behavior so the agent spends tokens only after an eligible GitHub issue exists.
close_reason: Implemented lean GitHub-issue-first roadmap: added issue watcher, parser, CLI command, eval/test migration, docs and planning updates.

title: Add production recovery verification loop
description: Back To Service currently detects Sentry issues and triggers repair, but production recovery is not complete until the correct Vercel project/domain is healthy. Add a post-repair verification loop that checks target Vercel deployment status, production domain HTTP health, Sentry issue quieting/resolution state, and GitHub issue status. Final decision should be recovered, still_failing, needs_human, or retry_repair. This should run after agent:sync/agent:run dispatch and be testable offline.
close_reason: Implemented target app config (productionUrl, vercelProjectId, sentryProjectSlug, healthCheck settings) and full verification loop (deploy check, HTTP health check, Sentry quieting check) with 23 new tests

title: Model explicit target app mapping
description: Add explicit configuration/state for each protected app: Sentry org/project/environment, GitHub owner/repo, Vercel project/team/domain, and production URL. The live SnapSync test showed multiple Vercel projects can deploy from one repo, so Back To Service must verify the exact project/domain that serves production instead of assuming any deployment success means recovery.
close_reason: Implemented target app config (productionUrl, vercelProjectId, sentryProjectSlug, healthCheck settings) and full verification loop (deploy check, HTTP health check, Sentry quieting check) with 23 new tests

title: Document live incident findings and current automation boundary
description: Write up the NODE-EXPRESS-3 live test: Sentry detected intentional frontend boot crash, Back To Service created/updated GitHub issue, Claude workflow triggered but was blocked by Anthropic credit balance, manual patch removed SENTRY_TEST_CRASH from snapsyncai, Vercel eventually restored production. Document what is automated today, what was manual, and what must be automated next for honest assignment/product documentation.
notes: Reopen: closed in error. Recovery loop landed in commit 5a74576 (agent:recover); the writeup itself still needs to happen.
close_reason: Completed README documentation of current production agent loop, assignment mapping, usage, evals, and limitations.

title: Unblock live Claude repair with Anthropic credits and production auth choice
description: Live Back To Service trigger reached Claude Code in snapsyncai, but run 25484040408 failed with Anthropic: Credit balance is too low. Decide whether production target workflow should keep demo GITHUB_TOKEN path, install official Claude GitHub App, or use a dedicated custom GitHub App token. Then rerun /back-to-service fix on LefterisXefteris/snapsyncai#1 and confirm a draft PR is created.

title: ingest Sentry production errors and sync GitHub issues
close_reason: Implemented and verified issue-first Sentry sync with Claude dispatch audit trail; existing incidents remain non-spammy and retry through recovery policy.
